trait.name: Bloodthirsty
entries: If the target of an attack has taken damage from another pirate this round, Captain Gail gains +1 to hit and damage on this attack (does not stack with other instances of Bloodthirsty).

trait.name: Protection of the Corpse Sea
entries: While in contact with seawater, Captain Gail has advantage on saving throws made against powers, spells, and other supernatural effects.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: Captain Gail makes two cutlass attacks whenever he uses the attack action.

action.name: Eldritch Blast
entries: {@atk rs} +9 to hit, range 120 ft., two targets. {@h} 5 ({@damage 1d10}) force damage. Each creature hit must succeed on a DC 15 Constitution saving throw or be pushed back 10 ft. in a straight line.

action.name: Cutlass
entries: {@atk mw} +8 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h} 8 ({@damage 1d8+4}) slashing damage.

action.name: Spellcasting
entries: Captain Gail is a spellcaster. His spellcasting ability is Charisma (spell save DC 19). He can cast the following spells:
{@b At will}: {@spell thaumaturgy}, {@spell mage hand}
{@b 3/day each}: {@spell create or destroy water}, {@spell misty step}, {@spell hold person}
{@b 2/day each}: {@spell tidal wave|xge}

legendary.name: Keelhaul the Bastards!
entries: All creatures hostile to Captain Gail within 10 ft. must succeed on a DC 15 Constitution saving throw or become {@condition restrained} as barnacles appear. They also take 8 ({@damage 2d8}) slashing and 10 ({@damage 2d10}) poison damage. On a success, they are not {@condition restrained} but still take half damage.

legendary.name: Forces of Karkinos, Aid Me.
entries: Hundreds of crabs swarm carrying Captain Gail up to 40 ft. without provoking opportunity attacks. All terrain within 20 ft. becomes difficult terrain. Enemies in it at the end of their turn take 9 ({@damage 3d6}) piercing damage.

legendary.name: Like Sharks, They Smell Blood.
entries: Any pirate allied with Captain Gail can move up to half their movement speed and make an attack with Advantage if Bloodthirsty would normally be triggered.

bonus.name: Flask of Seawater
entries: As a bonus action, Captain Gail pours seawater over himself, activating Protection of the Corpse Sea.

bonus.name: The Ocean Be Not Your Grave Today
entries: Captain Gail splashes a bit of water from his flask on an ally. That creature gains 12 ({@damage 1d10+7}) temporary hit points.

reaction.name: I’m Not the One You Should Be Fretting About
entries: After a creature attacks Captain Gail, he can grant each pirate within 5 ft. of the attacker one attack.

trait.name: Protection of the Corpse Sea
entries: The Sea protects her own and this Witch is one of her most devoted. While in contact with seawater, the Witch has advantage on saving throws made against powers, spells, and other supernatural effects.

action.name: Sea Rot (0th-Level Spell)
entries: {@atk rs} +6 to hit, range 120 ft., one target. {@h} 5 ({@damage 1d10}) necrotic damage. A stream of foul-smelling sea foam erupts from the caster’s hand, and spatters a target. Any creature damaged by this spell has their speed reduced by 5 ft. until the end of their next turn.

action.name: Spellcasting
entries: The Sea Witch is a spellcaster. Her spellcasting ability is Wisdom (spell save DC 14). She can cast the following spells:
{@b 3/day each}:
{@spell Wither and Bloom|scc}.

{@i Primordial Memory}. The Witch calls upon the ground's memory of the Primordial Sea. All creatures hostile within 60 ft. are caught in a deep sea current and pushed up to 30 ft. away from her. Each creature must succeed on a DC 14 Constitution saving throw or take 12 ({@damage 3d8}) force damage and be {@condition restrained} until the end of its next turn. On a successful save, the creature takes half damage and is not {@condition restrained}.

bonus.name: Premonitions of Decay
entries: A creature that the Witch can see which is in contact with seawater can be made to see brief glimpses of the distant future, where all life is extinguished, and the ocean is an endless grave. They must succeed on a DC 14 Wisdom saving throw or become {@condition frightened} of the water for 1 minute (saves at end of turn). A creature which passes this saving throw becomes immune to Premonitions of Decay until the end of their next long rest.

spell.name: Glitterdust

components.m: a pinch of powdered gold or silver

entries: A cloud of glittering particles appears around a point you can see within range.

Each object in a 10-foot-radius sphere within range is outlined in colorful light (your choice of color). Any creature in the area when the spell is cast is also outlined in light if it fails a Dexterity saving throw. For the duration, objects and affected creatures shed dim light in a 5-foot radius.
Additionally affected creatures or objects can't benefit from being invisible.
Creatures that fail their saving throw are also {@condition blinded} for the duration.
At the end of each of its turns, the target can make a Constitution saving throw. On a success, the spell ends for it.

spell.name: Vocalize

components.m: a small golden bell without a clapper

entries: You touch one willing creature. While this spell is active, the target is able to cast spells that normally require a verbal component without the caster needing to make a sound.
Such spells must be cast within the duration of the vocalize spell.

spell.name: Mykiel's Magic Missile
entries: You create four glowing darts of magical force. All darts hit one creature of your choice that you can see within range. A dart deals {@damage 1d4+1} force damage to its target. The darts all strike the target simultaneously.

spell.name: Minoletta's Shield

time.condition: which you take when you are hit by an attack or a harmful effect that requires a Dexterity saving throw to take half damage

entries: An invisible barrier appears in front of you and protects you.
Until the start of your next turn, you have a +1 bonus to Dexterity saving throws, including against the triggering attack.
You also instead take no damage if you succeed on the saving throw, and only half damage if you fail.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 4th level or higher, you can target one ally of your choice within 30 feet instead of yourself.

optionalfeature.name: Attaching
entries: As an automaton you can modify yourself with complex {@filter attachments|items|source=faa|type=ATTM}.
Without attaching such an item to yourself, you are unable to use its special properties, unless stated otherwise in the attachments item description
Attaching or unattaching an item requires an action, unless stated otherwise in the attachments item description.

entries.name: Attachment
entries: This item has been modified to attach to an automaton, see {@optfeature Attaching|FAA} for more.

item.name: Harpoon Gun
entries: A harpoon gun made for firing {@item Harpoon Spear|FAA}.

{@bold Special.} As part of your ranged attack made with the harpoon you may choose to tether yourself with a rope to the creature or object you hit; otherwise, you can pull the harpoon back to yourself as an object interaction.
 You need to use both hands to pull back the harpoon. If you choose to tether yourself, the hand you used to make the attack becomes occupied maintaining the tether. You cannot maintain multiple tethers in one hand, and creatures immune to the {@condition grappled} condition cannot be tethered.

Once per turn, you can make an opposed Strength check against a creature you have tethered (no action required). On a success, you can drag the tethered creature towards you using both hands. Doing so uses up 5 feet of movement for every 5 feet you drag it unless the creature is two or more sizes smaller than you.
 A tethered creature can attempt to do the same to you on its turn, though if you fail the opposed Strength check, you can choose to drop the tether rather than be dragged by it.
 Any time an opposed Strength check is made between you and a tethered creature, if both of you roll a 12 or higher on this check, the tether snaps.

Any creature within 5 feet of a tethered creature (including the tethered creature) can use an action to remove the harpoon. Doing so deals an additional {@dice 1d6} weapon damage to the creature as the harpoon is removed.
 Dealing 5 slashing damage to the tether (AC 10) also snaps it.

If you recover a harpoon spear or a harpoon gun bolt with a snapped rope, you can replace the rope over 10 minutes, using 1gp worth of rope and other supplies.
 This can be done over the course of a short or long rest.

item.name: Horn of Binding
entries: This ornate horn adorned with rubies can be bound to a willing creature.
The ritual takes 1 hour and needs 150 gp worth of rubies which the ritual consumes.

After a creature is bound to the horn, you can use an action to blow the horn. If the bound creature is within 15 miles of you, it knows your current location and feels compelled to come to you as quickly as possible.
Once you use the horn, it can't be used again until 7 days have passed.

item.name: Harpoon Gun Attachment
entries: This {@item Harpoon Gun|FAA} has been modified to attach to an automaton.

{@bold Special.} As part of your ranged attack made with the harpoon you may choose to tether yourself with a rope to the creature or object you hit; otherwise, you can pull the harpoon back to yourself as an object interaction.
 You need to use both hands to pull back the harpoon. If you choose to tether yourself, the hand you used to make the attack becomes occupied maintaining the tether. You cannot maintain multiple tethers in one hand, and creatures immune to the {@condition grappled} condition cannot be tethered.

Once per turn, you can make an opposed Strength check against a creature you have tethered (no action required). On a success, you can drag the tethered creature towards you using both hands. Doing so uses up 5 feet of movement for every 5 feet you drag it unless the creature is two or more sizes smaller than you.
 A tethered creature can attempt to do the same to you on its turn, though if you fail the opposed Strength check, you can choose to drop the tether rather than be dragged by it.
 Any time an opposed Strength check is made between you and a tethered creature, if both of you roll a 12 or higher on this check, the tether snaps.

Any creature within 5 feet of a tethered creature (including the tethered creature) can use an action to remove the harpoon. Doing so deals an additional {@dice 1d6} weapon damage to the creature as the harpoon is removed.
 Dealing 5 slashing damage to the tether (AC 10) also snaps it.

If you recover a harpoon spear or a harpoon gun bolt with a snapped rope, you can replace the rope over 10 minutes, using 1gp worth of rope and other supplies.
 This can be done over the course of a short or long rest.

item.name: Net Gun
entries: This Device is used to fire a {@item net|PHB}.
{@bold Special.} Firing a {@item net|PHB} from this, allows you to still use your remaining attacks, despite the limitations normally imposed by throwing a net.

item.name: Net Gun Attachment
entries: This arm replacement is used to fire a {@item net|PHB}, it attaches to an automaton.
{@bold Special.} Firing a {@item net|PHB} from this, allows you to still use your remaining attacks, despite the limitations normally imposed by throwing a net.

item.name: Harpoon Spear
entries: This Spear is designed to be fired from a {@item Harpoon Gun Attachment|FAA}

item.name: Swiftstrike Bracer Attachment
entries: This device can be attached to the arm of an automaton. You can use this bracer to hold a weapon that does not have the heavy or two-handed property. The bracer uses spring mechanisms to instantly retract or extend the weapon.

You can flick your wrist (no action required), to retract the armblade into your forearm or extend it from there. While it is extended, you can use the weapon as if you were holding it, and you can't use that hand for other purposes.
Switching the attached weapon is a delicate process and takes time (as if attuning to a magical item)
{@bold Special.} A weapon used as a {@italic Retractable Blade} has the following additional properties:

items: You cannot drop the weapon or be disarmed.
Drawing or stowing the weapon does not require an action.
While stowed the weapon is considered hidden.

item.name: Swiftstrike Bracer
entries: This device can be attached to the arm of a humanoid creature.

You can flick your wrist (no action required), to retract the armblade into your forearm or extend it from there. While it is extended, you can use the weapon as if you were holding it, and you can't use that hand for other purposes.
Switching the attached weapon is a delicate process and takes time (as if attuning to a magical item)
{@bold Special.} A weapon used as a {@italic Retractable Blade} has the following additional properties:

items: You cannot drop the weapon or be disarmed.
Drawing or stowing the weapon does not require an action.
While stowed the weapon is considered hidden.

item.name: Grappling Gun Attachment
entries: This wrist-mounted gadget can be activated as an action on your turn, firing out a reinforced cable onto a target surface within 60 feet of you.
Make a Dexterity check. You can add your proficiency bonus to this roll if you have proficiency with one of the following: Tinker’s Tools, Thieves’ Tools, or Mason’s Tools.

On a result equal to or higher than the structure’s AC (see {@table Object Armor Class|DMG}), the cable latches itself to the specified point, allowing you to swing across gaps or possibly climb steep walls.
You can have the cable detach itself from a surface as a bonus action, returning it to your device.

Additionally, you may choose to recall the cable without detaching it, pulling you towards the targeted surface before retracting completely. If the targeted surface is a Medium or smaller object, it is pulled towards you instead.

item.name: Grappling Gun
entries: This wrist-mounted gadget can be activated as an action on your turn, firing out a reinforced cable onto a target surface within 60 feet of you.
Make a Dexterity check. You can add your proficiency bonus to this roll if you have proficiency with one of the following: Tinker’s Tools, Thieves’ Tools, or Mason’s Tools.

On a result equal to or higher than the structure’s AC (see {@table Object Armor Class|DMG}), the cable latches itself to the specified point, allowing you to swing across gaps or possibly climb steep walls.
You can have the cable detach itself from a surface as a bonus action, returning it to your device.

Additionally, you may choose to recall the cable without detaching it, pulling you towards the targeted surface before retracting completely. If the targeted surface is a Medium or smaller object, it is pulled towards you instead.
